sentences.en: The weather today is way too hot!
I don't want to work — I just want to drink water.
I drank three cups of water and ate a big apple.
My dog is hot too and doesn't want to eat anything.
In the afternoon we won't go to the shop — we'll watch a movie at home.
What will the weather be like tomorrow? I think it won't be too hot!

quiz.q: How many cups of water did the speaker drink?
What does the dog not want to do?
What will they do in the afternoon?

grammar.e: 太…了 wraps around an adjective to say something is excessive — "way too…". It works for complaints and for strong praise.
grammar.x: 今天天气太热了！ — The weather today is way too hot!

grammar.e: 想 before a verb means "want to / would like to"; 不想 means you don't feel like doing it.
grammar.x: 我不想工作，我想喝水。 — I don't want to work; I want to drink water.

grammar.e: 了 right after a verb shows the action is finished, often with an amount after it.
grammar.x: 我喝了三杯水。 — I drank three cups of water.
